title: Registry good-standing oracle answer v1
description: Output of GET /api/registry/good-standing. A small, cacheable, Ed25519-signed answer a counterparty queries before transacting with an Argentine automated company. The `body` is offline-verifiable with `arg-verify attestation` (sig = Ed25519 over canonical(body); publicKey = standard-base64 SPKI). The ar-agents signature is CONVENIENCE; the load-bearing trust-minimization is body.attestation, which forwards the target's own publicly-anchored attestation + the witness anchor chain (verifiable against Bitcoin with no ar-agents key in the path).

operatorCuit.description: AUTHORITATIVE operator CUIT. Present ONLY for seed/independently-verified entries. A self-declared CUIT is NEVER here — it appears in selfDeclaredCuit, outside the trust claim.

selfDeclaredCuit.description: SELF-DECLARED, UNPROVEN CUIT for a self-listed entry. OUTSIDE the trust claim: treat as a hint, not an identity assertion. The oracle never resolves a ?cuit= query against this value.

basis.description: The honest SCOPE of this verdict, carried inside the signed body so the caveat travels with the offline-verifiable artifact: automated conformance of self-declared endpoints; NOT a solvency, identity, or fraud judgement.

attestation.description: Forwarded trust-minimized pointers. The load-bearing trust path, NOT the ar-agents convenience signature.

sig.description: Ed25519 over canonical(body), standard base64. Absent only when no signing key is configured.

publicKey.description: SubjectPublicKeyInfo (SPKI) DER, standard base64. Resolvable + pinnable at /.well-known/sociedad-ia/keys.
